what_it_does: This file is a structured interpretive analysis JSON for AXIS-REFACTOR.md: it extracts and normalises the plan, principles, axis roster, build order, carried scope, autonomy note, status log, and open questions about the universal axis-system refactor into observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, themes, embedding summaries, and questions.
observations: It is an interpretation layer over a markdown plan/status log, not source code.
It states the principle that every primary visual axis becomes a dedicated, hierarchical, typed registry and that component slots become subscriptions to axes.
It records that tokens are the canonical value-units of an axis and resolveCSS returns var(--token).
It lists a roster of axes (colour, space, size, motion, texture, depth, fill, form, symbol, meaning) with planned homes and statuses.
It includes an 8-step build order starting with axis-core and Motion de-owned from Glyphic.
It carries explicit do-not-lose scope: Foundry conversational UI, glyphic generate/save, consumer convergence, §7 open questions, event-sockets/addresses/conditions.
It contains an autonomy note that the author proceeds without Info/Tim sign-off and batches only genuinely blocking questions.
It logs status slices 60 and 61 as built and verified, including condition evaluator, event-sockets, 10 UI components, Glyphic as a Type, and glyphic-foundry verification.
It documents a deliberate non-move of legacy cv-shapes/cv-icons/cv-glyphics/cv-meaning libs to avoid breaking ~20 card references.
It references external slice numbers (59/60/61) and speakers (Info/Tim) whose full context is outside this file.
standouts: Promotes 'a facet' to a first-class Axis and 'a component slot' to a subscription — a generalisation that routes every visual value through an axis registry.
The clarification that tokens are the value-units of an axis, not wrapped or superseded by it.
The anti-drift mandate restated for axes: one home, subscriptions not copies, loud fail, interface as projection.
The deliberate non-move of legacy libs is reasoned and documented rather than silently drifted.
The autonomy note: proceed without sign-off, absorb found scope, batch only blocking questions.
conventions: Subscriptions not copies — consumers reference axis values.
Tokens as canonical value identity (resolveCSS → var(--token)).
Loud-fail on missing axis or value.
Verify-by-use each step (check_design_system clean; concrete resolve checks).
concerns: Named load-bearing gaps: full condition engine, socket/slot condition evaluator (conditions stored as strings only), event-sockets/addresses only partially built.
§7 design decisions (naming, v1 facet set, Form-axis ends, meaning vocabularies, taxonomy depth) are blocked awaiting Info/Tim sign-off.
Axis count is fluid: table lists ~9 axes plus Meaning, while log says 'all 9 axes built'.
References slice numbers and external speakers whose context lives outside this file.
notes: A machine-readable interpretive digest of the universal axis refactor plan, capturing principles, axis roster, build order, verified status, and deliberately named gaps.
questions: How many axes are canonical — 9 including Meaning, or 10, or 'every primary axis we discover'?
Is the condition/event-socket engine now fully built, or still in the declared-string-only / partial state recorded here?
Are the §7 naming and vocabulary questions still open awaiting Tim, or resolved in a later slice not present in this file?
What do slices 59/60/61 contain in full detail, since only summaries are referenced here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is the durable interpretive home for the axis-refactor plan and its status log, and it delivers principle, shape, roster, build order, carried scope, and verified status exactly as stated.
intention_signals: To turn a markdown plan/status log into a normalised, queryable interpretive record so the axis refactor's principles, roster, build state, gaps, and embedding summaries can be fused and reasoned about across the ledger.
novelty: Distinctive as an interpretive artifact that converts a design-system refactor narrative into a structured registry of axes, subscriptions, conventions, concerns, and embedding-ready summaries rather than leaving it as prose.
contribution: Contributes a normalised interpretation of the axis refactor: its principles, shared CV_AXIS shape, axis roster with homes/sources, build order, carried do-not-lose scope, verified status log, open questions, and dense embedding summaries — material a fused system can query without ranking or choosing winners.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive JSON for the ConceptV universal axis refactor plan, capturing the principle that every visual dimension becomes a typed CV_AXIS registry, tokens are value-units, component slots are subscriptions, the axis roster, build order, carried scope, and a verified status log.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to make the axis refactor plan and its build status machine-readable and queryable, preserving principles, gaps, and decisions for cross-file fusion.
